title: Links Hard and Soft
scenario: A deployment pipeline deploys v1.0 and v2.0 of an app under '/home/intern/releases/'. You need to manage application updates and backups using symbolic (soft) links and hard links. Create a symbolic link named '/home/intern/current' that points to the v2.0 release directory, and make a hard-link backup of the v1.0 configuration file.
symptoms: No hard link copy of app.conf in releases/v1.0/
mission: Create a symlink at ~/current pointing to releases/v2.0/ and a hard link at ~/releases/v1.0/app.conf.bak sharing the same inode as app.conf.

hints.title: Creating Symlinks
hints.content: Use `ln -s` to create a symbolic link: e.g. `ln -s target link_name`.

hints.title: Creating Hard Links
hints.content: Use `ln` without any flags to create a hard link: e.g. `ln source_file backup_file`.

hints.title: How to verify inodes
hints.content: You can verify that two files share an inode using `ls -i` or `stat -c %i`.

acceptance_criteria.description: ~/releases/v1.0/app.conf.bak is a hard link of app.conf sharing the same inode
